signals.headline: US and Iran trade strikes around strait of Hormuz as ceasefire is violated again
signals.summary: Iran accused the US of violating the agreement aimed at ending the war between the two countries and Donald Trump said he thought the ceasefire was over, after the US military launched strikes around the strait of Hormuz and revoked a temporary sanctions waiver for Tehran to export oil.
keyTakeaways: Trump added: “We attacked those very dangerous people affiliated with Iran last night, with great force … There’s something wrong with them.
Iran hit back at US bases in Bahrain and Kuwait.
Each side accused the another of reneging on the ceasefire agreement spelt out in the memorandum of understanding (MOU) signed on 17 June.
signals.whyItMatters: His remarks underline how close the ceasefire agreement between Iran and the US is to unraveling after Washington’s decision to launch strikes and revoke its oil-export sanctions waiver.

ja.headline: ホルムズ海峡周辺で米イランが相互攻撃、停戦合意は崩壊の瀬戸際に
ja.summary: 米軍がホルムズ海峡周辺への攻撃を実施し、イランへの石油輸出制裁の一時免除も撤回したことを受け、イランは停戦合意違反だと米国を非難した。トランプ大統領も停戦は終わったとの見方を示し、両国の緊張は一気に高まった。
keyTakeaways: トランプ氏は「イランと関係する危険な勢力を昨夜、強力に攻撃した」と述べ、強硬姿勢を鮮明にした。
イランはバーレーンとクウェートの米軍基地に反撃。
両国はそれぞれ、6月17日に署名された覚書に基づく停戦合意を相手側が破ったと主張している。
ja.whyItMatters: 米国による攻撃と制裁免除の撤回を受け、両国が署名した停戦合意は崩壊寸前の状態にある。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the ceasefire between the US and Iran... it sounds like it's falling apart?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It does. Both sides are accusing each other of breaking the agreement that was signed on June 17th.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What actually happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The US launched strikes around the strait of Hormuz and also revoked a temporary waiver that let Iran export oil. Iran then hit back at US bases in Bahrain and Kuwait.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And Trump thinks the ceasefire is over?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: He said he thought it was over. He also said the US attacked what he called very dangerous people affiliated with Iran.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why does this matter so much?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It shows how fragile the agreement is. The strikes and the decision to cancel the oil waiver have brought things right to the edge of unraveling completely.

signals.headline: Search under way after Boeing 737 cargo plane goes missing off Pakistan coast
signals.summary: K2 Airways said it was cooperating with the Pakistan Civil Aviation Authority and other ​government agencies.
keyTakeaways: Boeing did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
Flightradar24 tracking data showed the plane plunged about 5,000 feet in less than a minute, surged back 6,000 feet in just 30 seconds, before ‌a dive from 36,550 feet.
The missing aircraft is part of Boeing’s decades-old 737 family but is two generations older than the 737 MAX version that was involved in a recent safety crisis .
signals.whyItMatters: Early flight data indicated the 27-year-old converted freighter operated by K2 Airways from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates possibly crashed into the sea ⁠southwest of Karachi after a series of sharp altitude changes, before a steep final descent, according to flight-tracking service Flightradar24.

ja.headline: パキスタン沖でボーイング737貨物機が消息を絶つ、捜索続く
ja.summary: K2航空はパキスタン民間航空局および関係当局に全面協力していると表明した。ボーイング社からのコメントは得られていない。
keyTakeaways: 急激な高度変化が複数回記録されており、フライトレーダー24のデータでは1分未満で約1500メートル降下後、30秒で約1800メートル急上昇、その後約1万1000メートルから最終的な急降下に入った
機体はボーイング737シリーズに属するが、近年安全問題が相次いだ737MAXより2世代前の旧型
K2航空は当局と連携しながら捜索に協力している
ja.whyItMatters: フライトレーダー24のデータによると、機齢27年のこの貨物機はアラブ首長国連邦のシャルジャを出発後、急激な高度変化を繰り返したのち、カラチ南西の海上に墜落した可能性がある。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I'm seeing news about a cargo plane that's gone missing off Pakistan's coast?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. A Boeing 737 cargo plane operated by K2 Airways disappeared after flying from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates. Search efforts are now under way.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Do they know what happened to it?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Flight tracking data shows the plane went through some very unusual altitude changes — it dropped about five thousand feet in less than a minute, then climbed back six thousand feet in thirty seconds, before a steep final descent from over thirty-six thousand feet.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds alarming. Where did this happen?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Southwest of Karachi. The tracking data suggests it may have gone down into the sea there.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How old was this plane?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Twenty-seven years old. It's part of Boeing's older 737 family — two generations older than the MAX version that's been in the news for safety issues.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What's K2 Airways saying?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They've said they're cooperating with Pakistan's Civil Aviation Authority and other government agencies. Boeing hasn't commented yet.

signals.headline: World Cup 2026 quarterfinals: Full schedule and Egypt FIFA dispute
signals.summary: The FIFA World Cup quarterfinal lineup is complete after a dramatic round of 16 that delivered late comebacks, penalty shootouts and major upsets.
keyTakeaways: France, Morocco, Norway, England, Spain, Belgium, Argentina and Switzerland have all secured their places in the last eight, with four blockbuster quarterfinals set to be played across the United States.

Off the pitch, however, the tournament continues to be overshadowed by controversy, as Egypt alleges officiating bias following its defeat to Argentina and FIFA faces renewed scrutiny over decisions involving the USA team and intervention by US President Donald Trump.
Who is in the World Cup quarterfinals ?
signals.whyItMatters: The controversy comes amid heightened concerns over the integrity of officiating at the tournament after FIFA controversially rescinded Balogun’s red-card suspension following Trump’s public request.

ja.headline: 2026年W杯、準々決勝の顔ぶれと審判問題の影
ja.summary: 劇的な逆転劇やPK戦、番狂わせが相次いだラウンド16を経て、2026年FIFAワールドカップの準々決勝8チームが出揃った。
keyTakeaways: フランス、モロッコ、ノルウェー、イングランド、スペイン、ベルギー、アルゼンチン、スイスがベスト8に進出し、アメリカ各地で4試合の準々決勝が行われる。
ピッチ外では、エジプトがアルゼンチン戦の審判に偏りがあったと主張。さらにアメリカ代表に関するFIFAの判断やトランプ大統領の介入をめぐり、大会への批判が高まっている。
準々決勝に進んだのはどの8チームか。
ja.whyItMatters: トランプ大統領の公の要請を受けてFIFAがバログンの退場処分を取り消したことで、大会における審判の公正性への懸念はいっそう深まっている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the World Cup quarterfinals are all set?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Eight teams made it through—France, Morocco, Norway, England, Spain, Belgium, Argentina, and Switzerland. The matches will be played across the United States.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Sounds exciting. But I'm hearing there's some controversy too?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There is. Egypt has alleged officiating bias after losing to Argentina. They're disputing how the match was handled.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And there's something about the US team and President Trump?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. FIFA rescinded a red-card suspension for a player named Balogun after Trump publicly requested it. That decision has drawn a lot of scrutiny.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So people are worried about officiating integrity overall?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. These incidents have raised broader concerns about fairness and how decisions are being made at this tournament.

signals.headline: Meta’s glasses will turn off the camera if you tamper with the privacy light
signals.summary: The company is rolling out an update following increased scrutiny of its smart glasses.
keyTakeaways: Amid public backlash over its smart glasses, Meta announced that it will be updating its glasses with a new feature that will disable the camera when it detects that someone has tampered with or destroyed the glasses’ privacy LED light.
The update is meant to address modders who have taken actions such as physically drilling into the LED light .
Meta has previously tried to discourage tampering with the LED light.
signals.whyItMatters: Privacy concerns have also led some public venues to mull banning the devices.

ja.headline: プライバシーランプを壊すとカメラが停止——Metaがスマートグラスを更新
ja.summary: Metaのスマートグラスをめぐるプライバシー懸念が高まるなか、同社はカメラ機能に関する新たなアップデートを展開する。
keyTakeaways: プライバシー用LEDランプへの改ざんや破壊を検知した場合、カメラを自動的に無効化する機能が追加される。
ランプをドリルで物理的に破壊するなど、改造を試みるユーザーへの対応が主な目的だ。
Metaはこれまでも、ランプへの改ざんを控えるよう呼びかけてきた経緯がある。
ja.whyItMatters: プライバシーへの懸念は個人にとどまらず、一部の公共施設ではデバイスの持ち込み禁止を検討する動きも出ている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Meta's doing something new with their smart glasses?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. They're rolling out an update that will disable the camera if someone tampers with the privacy light.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: The privacy light — that's the little LED that shows when you're recording?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. It lets people nearby know the camera is on. But some users have been tampering with it, even physically drilling into it.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why would someone do that?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Presumably to record without others knowing. Meta's tried to discourage this before, but now they're taking a harder line.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So if the light's been messed with, the camera just won't work?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. The glasses will detect the tampering and shut the camera off entirely.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I guess privacy concerns have been pretty serious with these devices.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They have. Some public venues are even considering banning the glasses altogether because of those worries.

signals.headline: ABC tells the government to get out of its newsrooms
signals.summary: ABC is firing back at the Federal Communications Commission after the agency opened an investigation into The View ’s airtime of political candidates.
keyTakeaways: In February, the FCC Chair Brendan Carr confirmed that he’s reexamining The View ’s classification as a “bona fide” news program after the talk show held an interview with Rep.
James Talarico (D-TX), who is running for a spot in the Senate.
The View ’s classification exempts it from the “equal time rule ,” which requires broadcasters to give candidates running for the same office equal air time on the network.
signals.whyItMatters: In a letter to the FCC on Tuesday, ABC argues that the agency’s actions pose a risk to editorial independence by targeting programs “perceived as unfriendly to the current administration,” as reported earlier by The Wrap .

ja.headline: ABCがFCCに反論——報道の独立性を守れ
ja.summary: 米連邦通信委員会（FCC）がテレビ番組『The View』の政治的な放送内容をめぐる調査を開始したことを受け、ABCが正式に異議を唱えた。
keyTakeaways: FCCのブレンダン・カー委員長は2月、『The View』が「真正なニュース番組」として認定されているかどうかの再審査を表明
発端は、上院議員選に出馬中のジェームズ・タラリコ下院議員（民主党・テキサス州）への番組内インタビュー
この認定があることで、同番組は候補者に平等な放送時間を義務付ける「イコール・タイム・ルール」の適用外となっている
ja.whyItMatters: ABCは火曜日にFCCへ送った書簡の中で、今回の調査は「現政権に批判的とみなされた番組」を標的にするものであり、報道の独立性を脅かすと主張している。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw ABC is pushing back against the FCC. What's this about?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The FCC opened an investigation into The View — specifically whether it should still count as a real news program.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Why does that classification matter?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: News programs are exempt from the equal time rule, which says if you give one political candidate airtime, you have to offer the same to their opponents.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So what triggered the investigation?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The View interviewed Representative James Talarico, a Democrat from Texas who's running for Senate. The FCC Chair, Brendan Carr, is now reexamining whether The View qualifies for that news exemption.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And ABC isn't happy about this.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. In a letter to the FCC, ABC argues the agency is targeting programs it sees as unfriendly to the current administration — and that this threatens editorial independence.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So it's really about who gets to decide what counts as news.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. ABC is telling the government to stay out of its newsrooms.
